summary: Record the release lane architecture follow-up from the sealed machine release review; the current tree now has canonical converge/current/sealed lane separation and source-closeout vocabulary demotion.

observations.surface: mk/release.mk release-evidence-closeout-sealed, release-closeout-fixed-point, generated-artifact-converge, test-artifact-finalization, public-check-all-check, ops/reports/release-closeout-summary.json, ops/reports/release-closeout-batch-manifest.json, ops/operator/operator-release-summary.json
observations.problem: The sealed release path can reach sealed_clean_pass, but release evidence is still produced by multiple mutating canonical-report lanes. Operators can see a valid sealed batch/operator/check result beside a closeout-summary axis that still says unsealed_distribution_not_provided, and follow-up convergence can rewrite tracked evidence after seal-time checks.
observations.why_it_matters: The evidence is real, but the workflow makes release authority depend on remembering the correct lane order. Long-term release automation should separate report production, current-state verification, and immutable sealed-package verification so check targets do not double as refresh targets.
observations.automation_candidate: Introduce an explicit release transaction boundary: first converge canonical reports to a fixed point, then freeze a release evidence manifest/source ZIP, then run sealed verification against that frozen manifest in check-only mode. Rename or demote pre-distribution closeout-summary sealed fields so only batch/operator/sealed-check surfaces carry authoritative sealed_release_status.
observations.suggested_followup: Automated in this task family. release-evidence-converge is the mutating lane, release-verify-current and release-sealed-verify are check-only lanes, release-workflow-order-guard and Makefile static tests enforce terminal finality/fixed-point ordering, and closeout-summary now exposes pre_distribution_package_binding_status plus source_closeout_distribution_binding_status while keeping sealed_release_status only as a deprecated compatibility alias.
